skill.name: varlock-claude-skill
skill.description: Secure environment variable management ensuring secrets are never exposed in Claude sessions, terminals, logs, or git commits
skill.summary: Guidance for safer environment variable handling in AI coding sessions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The hardcoded URL findings are source repository references, not runtime network behavior. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives from descriptive text; no algorithm, code, script, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Source Repository URL
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs at SKILL.md lines 4 and 22. These are GitHub source references in metadata and documentation, not code that performs network requests or sends data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain only repository links. No executable network call, command, or data transfer behavior is present in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md lines 3 and 20 for a weak cryptographic algorithm. The text describes secure environment variable management and does not name or implement any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is descriptive prose only. There is no hashing, encryption, random generation, or cryptographic API usage to review.

content.value_statement: Secrets can leak through prompts, terminals, logs, and commits. This skill gives guidance for safer environment variable handling in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Guides safer handling of environment variables during AI coding sessions.
Focuses on preventing secrets from appearing in Claude sessions.
Highlights terminal, log, and git commit exposure risks.
Provides security-oriented patterns rather than executable automation.
Links users to the source repository for more information.
limitations: Does not include scripts, scanners, or automated secret detection.
Does not rotate, encrypt, or store secrets.
Does not verify whether a repository already contains leaked credentials.
Provides general guidance, not compliance certification.

use_cases.title: Review Prompt Safety
use_cases.description: Check whether planned AI prompts might reveal environment variables or secret values.
use_cases.target_user: Developers using Claude for code assistance

use_cases.title: Prepare Secure Debugging Sessions
use_cases.description: Plan terminal and logging steps that avoid printing tokens or credentials.

use_cases.title: Reduce Commit Risk
use_cases.description: Identify safer habits before adding configuration files or logs to git.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review before sharing context with an AI assistant.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this prompt for possible secret exposure. Tell me what to remove or replace before I send it.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Safe Debugging
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate debugging session with environment variables involved.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me debug this issue without printing environment variable values, tokens, or credentials in terminal output.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Commit Inputs
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before committing logs or configuration changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these planned files and commands for secret exposure risks before I commit anything to git.

prompt_templates.title: Design Secret Handling Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced team workflow for repeated AI-assisted development.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a secure workflow for using Claude, Codex, and Claude Code with environment variables across development, logs, and commits.

output_examples.input: I need to debug an API failure without exposing my token.
output_examples.output: The response lists safer debugging steps, redaction rules, and commands that avoid displaying secret values.

output_examples.input: I want to paste my configuration into Claude for help.
output_examples.output: The response identifies fields to remove, suggests placeholder names, and explains what context is still safe to share.

output_examples.input: I am about to commit a log file from a failed deployment.
output_examples.output: The response recommends reviewing the log for credentials, replacing sensitive values, and checking git changes before commit.

best_practices: Replace real secret values with clear placeholders before sharing context.
Check terminal output and logs before copying them into an AI session.
Review git changes for configuration files, logs, and generated artifacts.
anti_patterns: Pasting full environment files into a prompt.
Running commands that print all environment variables during debugging.
Committing logs or configuration files without checking for secrets.

faq.question: Does this skill run secret scans?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and patterns, but it does not include an automated scanner.

faq.question: Can it store or rotate secrets?
faq.answer: No. Use a dedicated secret manager for storing, rotating, and auditing credentials.

faq.question: Is it safe for production credentials?
faq.answer: It helps reduce exposure risk, but production credentials should never be shared with an AI assistant.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude Code support, and the guidance applies to AI coding sessions.

faq.question: What should I share instead of secret values?
faq.answer: Use placeholders, variable names, error messages without tokens, and sanitized configuration structure.

faq.question: Why were URLs flagged in the audit?
faq.answer: The static analyzer detected repository links. Review found no runtime network behavior.
